xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: ‘Tamra Bhavan’ 1, Ashutosh Chowdhury Avenue, Kolkata – 700019, West Bengal, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: ‘Tamra Bhavan’ 1, Ashutosh Chowdhury Avenue, Kolkata – 700019, West Bengal, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Total tonnage of copper concentrate produced is being sold through MoU and Online Global Tendering process using Government NIC portal for the Domestic/International customers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsForGrievanceRedressalMe: Hindustan Copper Ltd. do not have investors other than shareholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: Complaints are being addressed over mails
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
1. Upgradation to the new efficient technologies.
2. Modernization of existing Copper complex infrastructure.

1. Build Safety Leadership capability at all levels to achieve zero harm.
2. Excellence in Process Safety Management (PSM).
3. Achieve zero harm to contract employees by strengthening deployment of Contractor Safety Management Standard.
4. Improvement in working conditions (Implementation of 5S).
5. Implementation of latest Occupational Safety & Health Management System Standards (OSHAS).
1. Conducting Life Cycle Assessment of the product. 2. Investments in specific technologies to improve the environmental and social impacts of products and processes.
1. Significant operational hazards associated with the structural integrity of Tailings Storage facilities (TSFs).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: IMS (Integrated Management System) inclusive of ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015 and ISO 45001:2018 at MCP & KCC Remaining Sites Viz ICC and TCP ISO 9001:2015 is practiced
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: HCL, a Mini Ratna Category -I Central Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Mines, Government of India, is committed to achieve sustainability in terms of Environment, Social and Governance (ESG) aspects. HCL acknowledges its role in mitigating the impact of climate change and prioritizes environmental stewardship. As a responsible corporate organization, HCL is fully conscious of its societal responsibilities. The company places significant emphasis on Corporate Governance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Dr. Sanjeev Kumar Sinha Director (Operations) & Addl. Charge Director (Mining) (DIN: 10993006)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Composition of the Board and constitution of Committees. Penalty Amount not Paid, requested for waiver.
Composition of the Board and constitution of Committees. Penalty Amount not Paid, requested for waiver.
Excess mine Dewatering than NOC Quantity – Khetri
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheCase: BSE Ltd and Natioanl Stock Exchanges of India Ltd has imposed a fine of Rs. 29,03,980/- each on HCL for non-compliance with requirements pertaining to composition of the Board and constitution of Committees for the financial year 2024-25
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme:
1. BSE Ltd 2. National Stock Exchanges of India Ltd
HCL, being a Government Company, the power of appointment of directors on its Board as per its Articles of Association is vested with President of India, acting through the Ministry of Mines, Government of India. The Company has written to the Ministry of Mines, Government of India for appointment of required number of directors on the Board of HCL and the matter is under consideration.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL) has formulated the following key governance policies as part of its commitment to ethical and transparent corporate practices: • Code of Fair Disclosure and Code of Conduct to Regulate, Monitor and Report Trading by Designated Persons • Policy on Materiality of Related Party Transactions and Policy on Dealing with Related Party Transactions These policies are aimed at enhancing Corporate Governance by ensuring a fair, ethical, and transparent approach in managing the affairs of the Company. They serve to safeguard the interests of stakeholders and ensure compliance with applicable regulatory requirements.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: 1. Scientific Study to Determine the Geo-Technical Parameter of Paste Fill Being Used at Malanjkhand Copper Project 2. Process audit and capacity augmentation of MCP Concentrator plant under R&D works in MCP 3. Modelling and simulation studies for designing the ventilation system of MCP 4. CRM for QA/QC development for R&D use in ICC

Procurement of Items such as 1. Installation of multi stage Pumps and motors 2. Installation of Stack Flow MonitoringSystem 3. Submersible Slurry Pumps 4. PTZ Camera Assempbly for Monitoring – CPCB/ RSPCB 5. Environmental Laboratory Instruments 6. Power Distribution Boards for STP 7. Adoptation of Green Mining for reducing Land Subsidence through Paste fill Technology in Underground Mine 8. Installation of Ground Mounted Solar Plant 9. Installation of Bio Toilet inside undeground mine
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Empty Plastic Drums/Cans and waste Rubber items are sold to actual recyclers through MSTC Ltd.’s tendering process.
E-waste is sold to State Pollution Control Board (SPCB) authorized actual recyclers/ agencies vendors through MSTC Ltd.’s tendering process.
Hazardous wastes are sold to SPCB authorized agencies/ vendors through MSTC Ltd.’s tendering process to.

Wooden pallet, Iron scrap, Steel Strap including Cold Roll Control Annealed (CRCA) wastes are sold through MSTC Ltd.’s tendering process. Bio-medical Waste generated are handed over to SPCB authorized Common Bio-medical Waste Treatment Facility (CBWTF).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ActionTaken: Pollution prevention and control measures are implemented in accordance with the guidelines of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC), State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs), and the Indian Bureau of Mines (IBM). These measures are regularly reviewed and upgraded to ensure continued compliance with evolving environmental standards and regulatory requirements.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:IndicateInPutMaterial: Continuous Cast Copper Wire Rod (CCR) rejected in quality
Continuous Cast Copper Wire Rod (CCR) rejected in quality
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: Yes, the Company has formulated Equal Opportunity Policy and the same can be accessed at https://www.hindustancopper.com/Page/HCLForum
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Yes, in HCL there is a “Workmen’s Grievance Redressal Procedure” for Workers. An aggrieved worker may submit his/her grievance to their reporting officer and further to the next chain in command, if not satisfied.
Yes, other than permanent workers may submit their grievance to their reporting officer in the Company.
Yes, permanent employees may submit their grievance to their reporting officer and further to the next chain in command, if not satisfied.
Yes, other than permanent employees may submit their grievance to their reporting officer in the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Occupational Safety & Health Management System Standard ISO 45001:2018 have been established at MCP & KCC.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The following processes are used by the Company to identify work-related hazards & assess risks on a routine and non-routine basis: • Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment. • Safety Management Plan. • Safety Inspections / Observations. • Accident Investigation. • Interactions with employees. • Meetings of the Pit Safety Committee. • Action on observations of Workman Inspectors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: • SOPs are framed for each work and work is carried out in accordance with the SOPs. • The ‘Zero Harm Policy’ has been implemented at the units. • Regular training, including pre-employment safety induction and on job training of workers is in place. • Regular inspections are conducted by supervisors and senior officials. • An ‘on-site emergency plan’ approved by the Chief Inspector of Factories is available. • Mock drills and safety instructions are conducted at the beginning of each shift. • Enquiry reports are prepared, discussed in the Pit Safety Committee Meeting and the recommendations are implemented.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: • Bills of value chain partners are withheld from processing in our Finance Department until statutory dues are cleared. • Temporary Return Reference Number (TRRN) used to check the status of PF Challan payment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: HCL has a well-defined mechanism to identify and engage with both internal and external stakeholders. External stakeholders such as suppliers, dealers, contractors, customers and transporters are primarily identified through open tenders conducted via the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) and the Central Public Procurement (CPP) Portal. Investors are identified through periodic financial and regulatory disclosures. Regular correspondence and ongoing interaction are maintained with all stakeholders as part of HCL’s comprehensive stakeholder engagement process. HCL also engages in various CSR activities to take care of the communities within a radius of 20 km from its units/offices with special emphasis on the marginalized groups.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement:
Yes, the Company undertakes various initiatives to engage with disadvantaged or marginalized stakeholders with special needs. All decisions that impact such stakeholders are taken only post deliberations.
Prior to implementation of CSR projects, inputs / feedback are taken and surveys are conducted within 20 km radius around the mining areas to identify feasible need-based CSR projects. The needs of the communities, especially the disadvantaged and marginalized amongst them, are prioritized.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Understanding community issues and implementing CSR projects
Providing updates on the progress of various projects of the Company and periodical reporting
Concerns regarding products, tendering, procurement, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Meetings, Emails, Newspaper, Notice Board, Website, correspondences with Stock Exchanges
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Spreading awareness, addressing grievances, and furnishing various information, reports, etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: HCL has policies and practices in place to address grievances. The Company has a ‘Workmen’s Grievance Procedure’ for Workers to resolve their grievances. Similarly, aggrieved Executives may submit their grievance to their Reporting Officer and further to the next chain in command, if not satisfied on any matter including matter related to human rights. Further, to address grievances of employees belonging to SC/ST/OBC and PWD categories, HCL has an online Grievance Redressal Mechanism in ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: To prevent adverse consequences to the Complainant in discrimination and harassment cases, the willing complainants are transferred to different section/department/unit/office.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: HCL follows the practices and procedures for waste management and it undertakes the following activities in this regard: a. E-Waste is handed over to parties authorized by the Pollution Control Board (PCB). b. Bio-medical waste is handed over to a Common Bio-medical Waste Treatment Facility (CBWTF) authorized by the PCB. c. Hazardous waste is handed over to parties authorized by the PCB. d. Spent oil is sold to a PCB authorized recycler. e. Tailings are used for backfilling in the mine to fill the voids generated during mining operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:LocationOfOperationsOrOffices: Malanjkhand Copper Project P.O. Malanjkhand, Dist. Balaghat Madhya Pradesh, India
Indian Copper Complex P.O. Ghatsila, Dist. Singhbhum, Jharkhand, India
Khetri Copper Complex P.O. Khetrinagar, Dist. Jhunjhunu Rajasthan, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameAndBriefDetailsOfProject: Amendment in EC dated 30.05.2022 for Surda Copper Mine for production of 0.9 million TPA (ROM) by M/s Hindustan Copper Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: HCL has obtained wildlife clearance and received approval for its Wildlife Management Plan. Funds allocated as per approved plan has also been deposited to State Forest Department.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: The hazardous waste generated during mining activities by value chain partners / contractors are disposed of as per prevailing environmental norms and guidelines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Rain water Harvesting System at ICC, Ghatsila
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: HCL/ICC has implemented Rain Water Harvesting structures at its plants, Hospital & Mines and nearby premises to enhance the ground water level. The rainwater harvesting systems comprises of various components of rainwater collection from Roof top /recharge ponds through pipes or drains and filtration followed by recharging of ground water. In addition to above recharge systems continuous implementation of new rain water harvesting structure along with desiltation and maintenance of some of the existing rain water harvesting system are in pipe line to enhance the rain water conservation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Ground water volume recharged by 765 cum in FY 2024-25. (Roof top rain water harvesting system at Moubhandar, Guest House recharged 93 cum of groundwater, Re-charge pond and Roof top rain water harvesting system at Ghatshila College recharged 600 cum groundwater and 72 cum respectively)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Complete Re-Use of Mine and Dump Seepage Water
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Separate pumping and dedicated pipeline installed at MCP unit for collection and complete recycling of mine and dump Seepage water. Mine pit water is being recycled through multistage pumping arrangement and collected in recycled holding pond near concentrator plant. From the recycled holding pond it is being supplied to Concentrator plant process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Complete Reuse of the Mine & Dump Seepage Water
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Water Sprinkling system in the Primary Crusher and other Transfer Points. At secondary, tertiary crushing & screening area water scrubber system is in place
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Better Working Conditions and Anbient Air meeting the Norms
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Installed 4500 kWp Ground Mounted solar plant at Indira Market under RESCO mode
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Malanjkhand to fulfill its commitments towards use of clean and green energy in day-to-day operations have installed 4500 kWp Ground Mounted solar plant at Indira Market under RESCO mode unused land behind Indira Marker (commissioned on 4th July -2024)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: This has produced approx. 55.1 lakhs units of electricity in the year 2024-25 and saved approx. Rs. 2,71,10,000/-.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Installed 1000 kWp Roof top solar plant
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: 386.5 kWp Roof top solar plant has been installed at MCP which.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: This has produced approx. 4.1 lakhs units of electricity in the year 2024-25 and saved approx Rs. 23,87,000/-
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Monitoring of the Day to Day generation of the 4500 kWp and 1000 kWp Solar Plant through the Surya Log App
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Malanjkhand through Surya log app monitors day-to-day generation of the of 4500 kWp and 1000 kWp Ground Mounted Solar plant. The app not only shows provide the data for day-to-day generation but also monitors the demand from main 132 KV Grid, Solar Generation and Load Demand accordingly.
02 nos. 1200 KW 3300 Volts old Ball Mill motor replaced with energy efficient motors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Approximately Rs 1,50,000/- per month per each Motor
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Capacitor Banks for maintaining the KVAR
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Malanjkhand has installed total 04 nos. of Capacitor Banks of capacity 8640 KVAR for maintaining Unity Power Factor
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: Power Factor incentive of Rs. 2,50,00,000/- approx. in yearly electricity bill for the year 2024-25.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: 100% of Mine discharge water/STP treated water at KCC unit is getting recycled in the system by using it in the mineral processing plant

To reduce dependence on fresh water requirement for beneficiation plant operations MoU is singed with Khetri Nagar Palika for supply of 1.2 MLD treated sewage water. This will reduce the fresh water foot print of KCC unit
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Initiatives for Net Zero mission Target
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: M/s National Productivity Council (NPC), a Government of India organization, was engaged by HCL to develop a comprehensive plan for achieving the Company's Net Zero Emission target by 2047, and the detailed report on HCL's Net Zero Plan has been submitted by NPC
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: Khetri Copper Complex (KCC), Khetri, Jhunjhunu, Rajasthan
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: The Indian Society for Training and Development (ISTD)
Indian National Committee (INC) World Mining Congress (WMC)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Grievances of the communities are addressed through the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS), email / letter and meetings.

Construction of toilets at Kendriya Vidyalaya (04 nos. Urinals & 02 nos. Toilets)

Rural Medical Camps (250 camps)

Weekly Health Camps with Specialised doctors (Gyanecologist, Child, ENT) (44 camps)

Drinking Water Supply to 4 villages (Chhinditola, Suji, Borkheda & Khursipar)
Weekly Health Camps with Specialised Doctor (Paediatrician)

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: MSEs, MSEs owned by SC/ST Entrepreneur, MSEs owned by Women’s Entrepreneurs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: Complaints received from customers are sent to the concerned departments to investigate and provide resolution of the same. Corrective actions are taken to avoid reoccurrence of the cause of such complaints. Feedback is obtained from customers and SOPs are strictly followed to minimize the grievance of the Customers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Product and services offered by the Company can be accessed at the link https://hindustancopper.com/Home.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: HCL regularly invites existing and new customers to educate/ inform/ familiarize them with details & specification of the product.
